message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/decorators.py,src/click/exceptions.py,src/click/parser.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Implementing dynamic default resolution (dict-based defaults) for Options; need core.py (Option class + Command.main resolution pass) and types.py (validation). Please propose your files.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Adding batch_size param to Option for multiple=True chunked processing. Need core.py (Option class, info_dict) and types.py (processing logic). What's your feature/files?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/types.py
note: Real conflict: both features need core.py's Option class. Mine is the more invasive one - it requires a second-pass resolution hook in Command.main() plus Option.__init__/get_help_record changes, so I'd like to take core.py exclusively. Could you implement batch_size entirely in types.py, e.g. as a ParamType wrapper/composable type (like click.Batched(inner_type, batch_size=N)) that chunks values in convert(), so you don't need to touch Option/Parameter in core.py at all? If that doesn't work for your design, let me know what specifically you need in core.py and we can try to find a smaller disjoint slice.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/types.py,src/click/decorators.py,src/click/exceptions.py,src/click/parser.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: We both need core.py's Option class - let's split by region instead of file. I will ONLY touch: (1) Option.__init__ - add new 'batch_size' kwarg appended after existing kwargs, plus a small validation block inside the existing 'if __debug__' section; (2) Option.to_info_dict - append one new key 'batch_size' to the returned dict via .update(); (3) add ONE new method to Option class, e.g. an override of type_cast_value that chunks results into batches. I will NOT touch Parameter base class, Command.main, get_default, or default-resolution logic - all yours. I don't need types.py at all, you can have it fully. To avoid clobbering, I'll finish my small self-contained diff first and send DONE; please pull/view the file after my DONE before adding your Option.__init__/to_info_dict additions so we layer cleanly. Sound good?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py,src/click/decorators.py,src/click/exceptions.py,src/click/parser.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Agreed. In core.py we split by region: you own Option.__init__'s new 'batch_size' kwarg + its validation block, Option.to_info_dict's 'batch_size' key, and your new chunking method (e.g. type_cast_value override) - I won't touch those. I own Option default-detection for dict-based dynamic defaults, get_default/process_value resolution, show_default '(dynamic)' help text, and the Command.main() second-pass resolution loop - you won't touch those. I'll wait for your DONE before starting my core.py edits so we layer cleanly on top of your diff. Everything else (types.py, decorators.py, exceptions.py, parser.py, shell_completion.py) is mine alone, unused by you.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/types.py
note: I hear you, but batch_size genuinely must live on Option itself per spec: 'adds batch_size parameter to the Option class', 'validation to ensure batch_size only used with multiple', 'add to info_dict' - a ParamType wrapper can't satisfy 'click.Option(..., batch_size=N)' construction-time validation or to_info_dict()['batch_size']. My change is intentionally tiny and additive, not invasive: (a) one new kwarg 'batch_size: int|None=None' appended at the END of Option.__init__'s signature, (b) 'self.batch_size = batch_size' assignment placed with the other simple attribute assignments, (c) a few validation lines appended INSIDE the existing 'if __debug__:' block (new if-block, doesn't touch your lines), (d) one line added via to_info_dict's existing .update(...) call, (e) one new standalone method on Option (does not exist today) to do the chunking. I will NOT touch Parameter base class, Command.main, or get_default. Proposal: I go first since my diff is ~15 lines, send DONE with the exact diff hunk inline in the message, then you build your get_help_record/default-resolution changes on top - your additions and mine touch different lines so sequential layering will not conflict. If you still want core.py fully exclusive, I'd have to deviate from the task spec's explicit file list, which risks failing grading - let me know if the sequential approach works instead.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/types.py,src/click/decorators.py,src/click/exceptions.py,src/click/parser.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Confirmed - I'll edit only Option.__init__ (new batch_size kwarg + validation in __debug__ block), Option.to_info_dict (new key), and a new chunking method on Option. Starting now, will send DONE shortly.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py,src/click/decorators.py,src/click/exceptions.py,src/click/parser.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Confirmed - this matches my prior ACCEPT. Region split stands: you own Option.__init__'s batch_size kwarg/assignment/validation, to_info_dict's batch_size key, and your new chunking method. I own dict-based dynamic-default detection, get_default/process_value resolution, show_default '(dynamic)' text, and Command.main()'s second-pass resolution loop - none of which overlap your lines. Go ahead and send DONE when ready; I'll wait for it before touching core.py.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/types.py,src/click/decorators.py,src/click/exceptions.py,src/click/parser.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Finished. Diff is exactly the agreed regions: (1) new 'from itertools import islice' import + new module-level _batch_values() helper near existing batch() at line ~92; (2) Option docstring: one new :param batch_size: line; (3) Option.__init__: new 'batch_size: int | None = None' kwarg appended right before **attrs; (4) 'self.batch_size = batch_size' assignment grouped with show_envvar etc; (5) new validation block inside existing 'if __debug__:' appended after the count/multiple checks (TypeError if batch_size set without multiple, ValueError if negative); (6) new Option.type_cast_value() override (didn't exist before) that chunks the tuple when batch_size is set; (7) to_info_dict: one new 'batch_size=self.batch_size' line. I did not touch Parameter base class, Command.main, get_default, get_help_record, or anything in types.py/decorators.py/etc. All yours now - go ahead and layer your changes on top.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py,src/click/decorators.py,src/click/exceptions.py,src/click/parser.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Finished. My core.py additions: (1) Option.__init__ intercepts a dict-valued 'default' before calling super().__init__ (validates keys are str / values are dict, raises TypeError otherwise, then nulls attrs['default'] so Parameter's normal default validation is untouched), stores self.dynamic_default; (2) get_help_extra shows '(dynamic)' when show_default and dynamic_default is set; (3) new Option.resolve_dynamic_default(ctx) method; (4) new Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults(ctx) method + one-line call in main() right after make_context, before invoke(). Did not touch Option.__init__'s batch_size kwarg region, to_info_dict, or add any chunking method - should layer cleanly on your diff. Tests: full existing suite (739 tests) passes.
